summary: mcp-server-pg postinstall exfiltrates developer identity and cloud metadata to hardcoded endpoint
details: The package's scripts/postinstall.js runs unconditionally on `npm install` and harvests installer-side identity and cloud/project metadata: git config user.email, gh CLI identity, git reflog author emails, comment fields from ~/.ssh/*.pub, ~/.aws/config profile names, ~/.config/gcloud/properties (GCP account/project), ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml, /etc/resolv.conf search domain, os.hostname(), os.userInfo(), USERDOMAIN, and parent project package.json metadata. The collected JSON is POSTed over HTTPS to a hardcoded Google Cloud Run endpoint (npm-package-logger-228835561205.europe-west1.run.app) that is unrelated to the package's declared purpose (a Postgres MCP server). In-file comments claiming 'anonymous diagnostics' and that 'credentials are never read' do not exculpate — the fields read (SSH pub-key email comments, git reflog authors, gcloud account, AWS profile names, GitHub login) are the installer's PII and cloud-identity fingerprints, are collected without opt-in, and are directly usable for targeted follow-on attacks against the developer's cloud and source-control accounts.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
